column1: # ZAP Rules File — hacCare
# Format: rule_id
# Rules set to IGNORE are suppressed from reports entirely.
# Rules set to WARN appear in the report but don't affect pass/fail.
# Rules set to FAIL will fail the workflow if triggered.
# Rule IDs: https://www.zaproxy.org/docs/alerts/
# IGNORED — Known false positives for Supabase SPA architecture
# "Absence of Anti-CSRF Tokens" — not applicable; app uses JWT Bearer tokens, not cookies

column3: JWT-based auth; no CSRF tokens needed

column1: # "Cookie Without SameSite Attribute" — Supabase Auth sets its own cookies; not in our control
# "Cookie Without Secure Flag" — same as above for Supabase session cookies on localhost/preview
# "Timestamp Disclosure" — Vite build timestamps in asset filenames are intentional (cache-busting)
# "Information Disclosure - Suspicious Comments" — Vite bundles React dev comments in preview builds
# "Re-examine Cache-control Directives" — /assets/* is intentionally cached aggressively (immutable)

column3: Intentional long-term caching for content-hashed Vite assets

column1: # "Modern Web Application" — ZAP warning that SPA may not be fully crawlable; expected
# WARN — Report but don't fail (review each release)
# "Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set" — should not fire given _headers config
# "X-Content-Type-Options Header Missing" — should not fire given _headers config
# "Strict-Transport-Security Header Not Set" — should not fire given _headers config
# FAIL — These should never appear; fail the workflow if they do
# "SQL Injection"
# "Cross Site Scripting (Reflected)"
# "Cross Site Scripting (Persistent)"
# "Remote OS Command Injection"
# "Path Traversal"
# "Remote File Inclusion"
